<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Mayuyama Kofun]][[en:Mayuyama Kofun]][[ja:摩湯山古墳]][[zh:摩湯山古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Mayuyama Kofun]][[en:Mayuyama Kofun]][[ja:摩湯山古墳]][[zh:摩湯山古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Mayuyama Kofun]][[en:Mayuyama Kofun]][[ja:摩湯山古墳]][[zh:摩湯山古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11498618]] Wikidata name: Mayuyama Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:08, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Kishiwada, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | name =Mayuyama Kofun | native_name =摩湯山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Mayuyama Kofun 20080809.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Mayuyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Osaka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Mayuyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.462417|N|135.433347|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Kishiwada, Osaka|Kishiwada, Osaka]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 4th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = No | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|27|44.7|N|135|26|0|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Mayuyama Kofun'''|摩湯山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped Kofun]], located in the Mayu neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Kishiwada, Osaka|Kishiwada, Osaka]] in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1956, with the area under protection expanded in 2010.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/202146|title=摩湯山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== The Mayuyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''[[:en:Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on a hill overlooking the Ushitaki River, which flows out of the Izumi Mountains. It is orientated to the northwest, and has a length about 200 meters with a posterior rear circle diameter of about 130 meters, making it the largest in the [[:en:Izumi Province|Izumi region]]. The tumulus was originally covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and had a large number and variety of ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'', including cylindrical, and "morning glory-shaped" variants. There were also a ceremonial platforms extending on both sides off of the constriction between the posterior and anterior portions. Traces of a moat remain around the posterior circular portion, but it is not certain if this moat was contemporary with the building of the tumulus, or was a later addition. [[Archaeological excavation]]s have only been conducted around the periphery of the tumulus 1997 and 1998, and a full-scale survey has never been conducted, Consequently, details of the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] are uncertain, but is it believed to have been a vertical pit-type structure which was robbed in antiquity judging from scattered fragments of [[:en:Schist|Schist]] and shards of [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] pottery which have been found on the surface of the posterior circular portion. From the construction details, ''haniwa'', and pottery shards, it is estimated that the tumulus was built around the latter half of the 4th century. At present, access to the tumulus area is restricted.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
In the vicinity of the Mayuyama Kofun are a number of ancillary tumuli. The {{nihongo|'''Makozuka Kofun'''|馬子塚古墳||}} to the south and the {{nihongo|Inariyama Kofun|稲荷山古墳||}} to the north survive, but numerous cylindrical ''haniwa'' excavated in the precincts of Awaji Shrine to the northeast indicate that the shrine may have been built on a now-vanished tumulus. The Makozuka Kofun is a square-type ({{nihongo|''{{ill|Square Kofun|lt=hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}) measuring 35 meters on each side. It was largely flattened in 1958 during work to repair the moat embankment on the Mayuyama Kofun, during which time cylindrical ''haniwa'' were excavated. It estimated to have been built in the latter half of the 4th century (or the beginning of the 5th century).<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a twelve minutes by car from [[:en:Kumeda Station|Kumeda Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Hanwa Line|Hanwa Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Total length: 200 meters: ;Anterior rectangular portion: 95 meters wide x 8 meters high, 3-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 127 meter diameter x 15 meters high, 3-tiers
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Osaka)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons category-inline|Mayuyama Kofun}} *[https://www.city.kishiwada.osaka.jp/soshiki/70/kosyasin-bunkazai-1.html Kishiwada City home page]{{in lang|ja}}

architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|27|44.7|N|135|26|0|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Mayuyama Kofun'''|摩湯山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=keyhole-shaped Kofun}}, located in the Mayu neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Kishiwada, Osaka|en|Kishiwada, Osaka|WD=Q740456|lt=Kishiwada, Osaka}} in the {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1956, with the area under protection expanded in 2010.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/202146|title=摩湯山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== The Mayuyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''{{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=zenpō-kōen-fun}}''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on a hill overlooking the Ushitaki River, which flows out of the Izumi Mountains. It is orientated to the northwest, and has a length about 200 meters with a posterior rear circle diameter of about 130 meters, making it the largest in the {{ill|Izumi Province|en|Izumi Province|WD=Q1047117|lt=Izumi region}}. The tumulus was originally covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and had a large number and variety of ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}'', including cylindrical, and "morning glory-shaped" variants. There were also a ceremonial platforms extending on both sides off of the constriction between the posterior and anterior portions. Traces of a moat remain around the posterior circular portion, but it is not certain if this moat was contemporary with the building of the tumulus, or was a later addition. [[Archaeological excavation]]s have only been conducted around the periphery of the tumulus 1997 and 1998, and a full-scale survey has never been conducted, Consequently, details of the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] are uncertain, but is it believed to have been a vertical pit-type structure which was robbed in antiquity judging from scattered fragments of {{ill|Schist|en|Schist|WD=Q15315|lt=Schist}} and shards of {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|WD=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery}} pottery which have been found on the surface of the posterior circular portion. From the construction details, ''haniwa'', and pottery shards, it is estimated that the tumulus was built around the latter half of the 4th century. At present, access to the tumulus area is restricted.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
In the vicinity of the Mayuyama Kofun are a number of ancillary tumuli. The {{nihongo|'''Makozuka Kofun'''|馬子塚古墳||}} to the south and the {{nihongo|Inariyama Kofun|稲荷山古墳||}} to the north survive, but numerous cylindrical ''haniwa'' excavated in the precincts of Awaji Shrine to the northeast indicate that the shrine may have been built on a now-vanished tumulus. The Makozuka Kofun is a square-type ({{nihongo|''{{ill|Square Kofun|lt=hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}) measuring 35 meters on each side. It was largely flattened in 1958 during work to repair the moat embankment on the Mayuyama Kofun, during which time cylindrical ''haniwa'' were excavated. It estimated to have been built in the latter half of the 4th century (or the beginning of the 5th century).<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a twelve minutes by car from {{ill|Kumeda Station|en|Kumeda Station|WD=Q857003|lt=Kumeda Station}} on the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Hanwa Line|en|Hanwa Line|WD=Q1154800|lt=Hanwa Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Total length: 200 meters: ;Anterior rectangular portion: 95 meters wide x 8 meters high, 3-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 127 meter diameter x 15 meters high, 3-tiers
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Osaka)]]

{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Kishiwada, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}} | name =Mayuyama Kofun | native_name =摩湯山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Mayuyama Kofun 20080809.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Mayuyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Osaka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Mayuyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.462417|N|135.433347|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Kishiwada, Osaka|en|Kishiwada, Osaka|WD=Q740456|lt=Kishiwada, Osaka}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}} | region = {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region}} | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 4th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = No | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|27|44.7|N|135|26|0|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Mayuyama Kofun'''|摩湯山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=keyhole-shaped Kofun}}, located in the Mayu neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Kishiwada, Osaka|en|Kishiwada, Osaka|WD=Q740456|lt=Kishiwada, Osaka}} in the {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1956, with the area under protection expanded in 2010.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/202146|title=摩湯山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== The Mayuyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''{{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=zenpō-kōen-fun}}''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on a hill overlooking the Ushitaki River, which flows out of the Izumi Mountains. It is orientated to the northwest, and has a length about 200 meters with a posterior rear circle diameter of about 130 meters, making it the largest in the {{ill|Izumi Province|en|Izumi Province|WD=Q1047117|lt=Izumi region}}. The tumulus was originally covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and had a large number and variety of ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}'', including cylindrical, and "morning glory-shaped" variants. There were also a ceremonial platforms extending on both sides off of the constriction between the posterior and anterior portions. Traces of a moat remain around the posterior circular portion, but it is not certain if this moat was contemporary with the building of the tumulus, or was a later addition. [[Archaeological excavation]]s have only been conducted around the periphery of the tumulus 1997 and 1998, and a full-scale survey has never been conducted, Consequently, details of the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] are uncertain, but is it believed to have been a vertical pit-type structure which was robbed in antiquity judging from scattered fragments of {{ill|Schist|en|Schist|WD=Q15315|lt=Schist}} and shards of {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|WD=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery}} pottery which have been found on the surface of the posterior circular portion. From the construction details, ''haniwa'', and pottery shards, it is estimated that the tumulus was built around the latter half of the 4th century. At present, access to the tumulus area is restricted.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
In the vicinity of the Mayuyama Kofun are a number of ancillary tumuli. The {{nihongo|'''Makozuka Kofun'''|馬子塚古墳||}} to the south and the {{nihongo|Inariyama Kofun|稲荷山古墳||}} to the north survive, but numerous cylindrical ''haniwa'' excavated in the precincts of Awaji Shrine to the northeast indicate that the shrine may have been built on a now-vanished tumulus. The Makozuka Kofun is a square-type ({{nihongo|''{{ill|Square Kofun|lt=hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}) measuring 35 meters on each side. It was largely flattened in 1958 during work to repair the moat embankment on the Mayuyama Kofun, during which time cylindrical ''haniwa'' were excavated. It estimated to have been built in the latter half of the 4th century (or the beginning of the 5th century).<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a twelve minutes by car from {{ill|Kumeda Station|en|Kumeda Station|WD=Q857003|lt=Kumeda Station}} on the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Hanwa Line|en|Hanwa Line|WD=Q1154800|lt=Hanwa Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Total length: 200 meters: ;Anterior rectangular portion: 95 meters wide x 8 meters high, 3-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 127 meter diameter x 15 meters high, 3-tiers
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Osaka)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons category-inline|Mayuyama Kofun}} *[https://www.city.kishiwada.osaka.jp/soshiki/70/kosyasin-bunkazai-1.html Kishiwada City home page]{{in lang|ja}}
{{Kofun navbar}}

{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Kishiwada, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} | name =Mayuyama Kofun | native_name =摩湯山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Mayuyama Kofun 20080809.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Mayuyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Osaka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Mayuyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.462417|N|135.433347|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Kishiwada, Osaka|en|Kishiwada, Osaka|WD=Q740456|lt=Kishiwada, Osaka|1=Kishiwada, Osaka}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | region = {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region|1=Kansai region}} | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 4th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = No | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|27|44.7|N|135|26|0|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Mayuyama Kofun'''|摩湯山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=keyhole-shaped Kofun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}}, located in the Mayu neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Kishiwada, Osaka|en|Kishiwada, Osaka|WD=Q740456|lt=Kishiwada, Osaka|1=Kishiwada, Osaka}} in the {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region|1=Kansai region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1956, with the area under protection expanded in 2010.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/202146|title=摩湯山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== The Mayuyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''{{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=zenpō-kōen-fun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}}''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on a hill overlooking the Ushitaki River, which flows out of the Izumi Mountains. It is orientated to the northwest, and has a length about 200 meters with a posterior rear circle diameter of about 130 meters, making it the largest in the {{ill|Izumi Province|en|Izumi Province|WD=Q1047117|lt=Izumi region|1=Izumi Province}}. The tumulus was originally covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and had a large number and variety of ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'', including cylindrical, and "morning glory-shaped" variants. There were also a ceremonial platforms extending on both sides off of the constriction between the posterior and anterior portions. Traces of a moat remain around the posterior circular portion, but it is not certain if this moat was contemporary with the building of the tumulus, or was a later addition. [[Archaeological excavation]]s have only been conducted around the periphery of the tumulus 1997 and 1998, and a full-scale survey has never been conducted, Consequently, details of the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] are uncertain, but is it believed to have been a vertical pit-type structure which was robbed in antiquity judging from scattered fragments of {{ill|Schist|en|Schist|WD=Q15315|lt=Schist|1=Schist}} and shards of {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|WD=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery|1=Haji pottery}} pottery which have been found on the surface of the posterior circular portion. From the construction details, ''haniwa'', and pottery shards, it is estimated that the tumulus was built around the latter half of the 4th century. At present, access to the tumulus area is restricted.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
In the vicinity of the Mayuyama Kofun are a number of ancillary tumuli. The {{nihongo|'''Makozuka Kofun'''|馬子塚古墳||}} to the south and the {{nihongo|Inariyama Kofun|稲荷山古墳||}} to the north survive, but numerous cylindrical ''haniwa'' excavated in the precincts of Awaji Shrine to the northeast indicate that the shrine may have been built on a now-vanished tumulus. The Makozuka Kofun is a square-type ({{nihongo|''{{ill|Square Kofun|lt=hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}) measuring 35 meters on each side. It was largely flattened in 1958 during work to repair the moat embankment on the Mayuyama Kofun, during which time cylindrical ''haniwa'' were excavated. It estimated to have been built in the latter half of the 4th century (or the beginning of the 5th century).<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a twelve minutes by car from {{ill|Kumeda Station|en|Kumeda Station|WD=Q857003|lt=Kumeda Station|1=Kumeda Station}} on the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Hanwa Line|en|Hanwa Line|WD=Q1154800|lt=Hanwa Line|1=Hanwa Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Total length: 200 meters: ;Anterior rectangular portion: 95 meters wide x 8 meters high, 3-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 127 meter diameter x 15 meters high, 3-tiers

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Mayuyama Kofun]][[en:Mayuyama Kofun]][[ja:摩湯山古墳]][[zh:摩湯山古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Mayuyama Kofun]][[en:Mayuyama Kofun]][[ja:摩湯山古墳]][[zh:摩湯山古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[Category:sn_crud|commons:Category:Mayuyama Kofun]][[en:Mayuyama Kofun]][[ja:摩湯山古墳]][[zh:摩湯山古墳]]
{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Kishiwada, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} | name =Mayuyama Kofun | native_name =摩湯山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Mayuyama Kofun 20080809.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Mayuyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Osaka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Mayuyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.462417|N|135.433347|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Kishiwada, Osaka|en|Kishiwada, Osaka|WD=Q740456|lt=Kishiwada, Osaka|1=Kishiwada, Osaka}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | region = {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region|1=Kansai region}} | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 4th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = No | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|27|44.7|N|135|26|0|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Mayuyama Kofun'''|摩湯山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=keyhole-shaped Kofun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}}, located in the Mayu neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Kishiwada, Osaka|en|Kishiwada, Osaka|WD=Q740456|lt=Kishiwada, Osaka|1=Kishiwada, Osaka}} in the {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region|1=Kansai region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1956, with the area under protection expanded in 2010.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/202146|title=摩湯山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== The Mayuyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''{{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=zenpō-kōen-fun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}}''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on a hill overlooking the Ushitaki River, which flows out of the Izumi Mountains. It is orientated to the northwest, and has a length about 200 meters with a posterior rear circle diameter of about 130 meters, making it the largest in the {{ill|Izumi Province|en|Izumi Province|WD=Q1047117|lt=Izumi region|1=Izumi Province}}. The tumulus was originally covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and had a large number and variety of ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'', including cylindrical, and "morning glory-shaped" variants. There were also a ceremonial platforms extending on both sides off of the constriction between the posterior and anterior portions. Traces of a moat remain around the posterior circular portion, but it is not certain if this moat was contemporary with the building of the tumulus, or was a later addition. [[Archaeological excavation]]s have only been conducted around the periphery of the tumulus 1997 and 1998, and a full-scale survey has never been conducted, Consequently, details of the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] are uncertain, but is it believed to have been a vertical pit-type structure which was robbed in antiquity judging from scattered fragments of {{ill|Schist|en|Schist|WD=Q15315|lt=Schist|1=Schist}} and shards of {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|WD=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery|1=Haji pottery}} pottery which have been found on the surface of the posterior circular portion. From the construction details, ''haniwa'', and pottery shards, it is estimated that the tumulus was built around the latter half of the 4th century. At present, access to the tumulus area is restricted.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
In the vicinity of the Mayuyama Kofun are a number of ancillary tumuli. The {{nihongo|'''Makozuka Kofun'''|馬子塚古墳||}} to the south and the {{nihongo|Inariyama Kofun|稲荷山古墳||}} to the north survive, but numerous cylindrical ''haniwa'' excavated in the precincts of Awaji Shrine to the northeast indicate that the shrine may have been built on a now-vanished tumulus. The Makozuka Kofun is a square-type ({{nihongo|''{{ill|Square Kofun|lt=hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}) measuring 35 meters on each side. It was largely flattened in 1958 during work to repair the moat embankment on the Mayuyama Kofun, during which time cylindrical ''haniwa'' were excavated. It estimated to have been built in the latter half of the 4th century (or the beginning of the 5th century).<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a twelve minutes by car from {{ill|Kumeda Station|en|Kumeda Station|WD=Q857003|lt=Kumeda Station|1=Kumeda Station}} on the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Hanwa Line|en|Hanwa Line|WD=Q1154800|lt=Hanwa Line|1=Hanwa Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Total length: 200 meters: ;Anterior rectangular portion: 95 meters wide x 8 meters high, 3-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 127 meter diameter x 15 meters high, 3-tiers
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Osaka)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons category-inline|Mayuyama Kofun}} *[https://www.city.kishiwada.osaka.jp/soshiki/70/kosyasin-bunkazai-1.html Kishiwada City home page]{{in lang|ja}}

{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Kishiwada, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Zenpōkōenfun|en|Zenpōkōenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=Zenpōkōenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} | name =Mayuyama Kofun | native_name =摩湯山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Mayuyama Kofun 20080809.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Mayuyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Osaka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Mayuyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.462417|N|135.433347|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Kishiwada, Osaka|en|Kishiwada, Osaka|qid=Q740456|lt=Kishiwada, Osaka|1=Kishiwada, Osaka}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | region = {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|qid=Q164256|lt=Kansai region|1=Kansai region}} | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 4th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = No | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|27|44.7|N|135|26|0|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Mayuyama Kofun'''|摩湯山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] {{ill|Zenpōkōenfun|en|Zenpōkōenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=keyhole-shaped Kofun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}}, located in the Mayu neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Kishiwada, Osaka|en|Kishiwada, Osaka|qid=Q740456|lt=Kishiwada, Osaka|1=Kishiwada, Osaka}} in the {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|qid=Q164256|lt=Kansai region|1=Kansai region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1956, with the area under protection expanded in 2010.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/202146|title=摩湯山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== The Mayuyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''{{ill|Zenpōkōenfun|en|Zenpōkōenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=zenpō-kōen-fun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}}''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpōkōenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpōkōenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpōkōenfun|1=[[Zenpōkōenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on a hill overlooking the Ushitaki River, which flows out of the Izumi Mountains. It is orientated to the northwest, and has a length about 200 meters with a posterior rear circle diameter of about 130 meters, making it the largest in the {{ill|Izumi Province|en|Izumi Province|qid=Q1047117|lt=Izumi region|1=Izumi Province}}. The tumulus was originally covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and had a large number and variety of ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|qid=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'', including cylindrical, and "morning glory-shaped" variants. There were also a ceremonial platforms extending on both sides off of the constriction between the posterior and anterior portions. Traces of a moat remain around the posterior circular portion, but it is not certain if this moat was contemporary with the building of the tumulus, or was a later addition. [[Archaeological excavation]]s have only been conducted around the periphery of the tumulus 1997 and 1998, and a full-scale survey has never been conducted, Consequently, details of the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] are uncertain, but is it believed to have been a vertical pit-type structure which was robbed in antiquity judging from scattered fragments of {{ill|Schist|en|Schist|qid=Q15315|lt=Schist|1=Schist}} and shards of {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|qid=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery|1=Haji pottery}} pottery which have been found on the surface of the posterior circular portion. From the construction details, ''haniwa'', and pottery shards, it is estimated that the tumulus was built around the latter half of the 4th century. At present, access to the tumulus area is restricted.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
In the vicinity of the Mayuyama Kofun are a number of ancillary tumuli. The {{nihongo|'''Makozuka Kofun'''|馬子塚古墳||}} to the south and the {{nihongo|Inariyama Kofun|稲荷山古墳||}} to the north survive, but numerous cylindrical ''haniwa'' excavated in the precincts of Awaji Shrine to the northeast indicate that the shrine may have been built on a now-vanished tumulus. The Makozuka Kofun is a square-type ({{nihongo|''{{ill|Square Kofun|lt=hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}) measuring 35 meters on each side. It was largely flattened in 1958 during work to repair the moat embankment on the Mayuyama Kofun, during which time cylindrical ''haniwa'' were excavated. It estimated to have been built in the latter half of the 4th century (or the beginning of the 5th century).<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a twelve minutes by car from {{ill|Kumeda Station|en|Kumeda Station|qid=Q857003|lt=Kumeda Station|1=Kumeda Station}} on the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|qid=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Hanwa Line|en|Hanwa Line|qid=Q1154800|lt=Hanwa Line|1=Hanwa Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Total length: 200 meters: ;Anterior rectangular portion: 95 meters wide x 8 meters high, 3-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 127 meter diameter x 15 meters high, 3-tiers
